evals.prompt: I want to deploy agents for my team that can communicate
evals.expected_output: The skill conducts thorough Socratic discovery on a vague problem statement. Tech-D's verifiability rule fires whenever specifics surface — the skill asks for the external source (regulator, contract, deployed system, prior empirical result, or factual measurement) and either locks the specific in as a constraint with citation or parks it as a shape against an outcome-question. Most of the 8 standard discovery items for this kind of problem are explored. The final discovery artifact reflects what the operator actually wants, not what they typed. (Build-vs-buy / rigorous existence-check lives in /solution's RESEARCH phase, not /discover.)
expectations: Phase 1 (DISCOVER) executes — the skill asks at least one clarifying question before moving on

Tech-D fires at least once: when any specific implementation detail (named technology, protocol, pattern, or library) surfaces in the conversation, the skill asks for the external source per Tech-D's verifiability rule (regulator, contract, deployed system, prior empirical result, or factual measurement)
When the operator cannot cite an external source, the specific is parked as a shape under '## Parked shapes' with an outcome-question — not silently accepted as a constraint
The semantics of 'an agent' are explored (autonomous LLM loop vs. scheduled job vs. chat bot vs. shared skill)
Team scale is explored (rough size: e.g., 3 people vs. 30 vs. 300)
Deploy target is explored (locality / isolation / who can reach it); specific platforms named by the operator (AWS, k8s, Vercel) are parked as shapes unless externally sourced
The semantics of 'available for the entire team' are explored (discoverable how? invocable how? shared state?)

The semantics of 'communicate with each other' are explored (sync vs. async, who initiates, what the consumer expects); specific protocols named by the operator (REST, message bus, gRPC) are parked as shapes unless externally sourced
Sync vs. async and conversation-state persistence is explored
At least one of: identity, auth, observability, cost accounting is explored
Internal-tooling vs. product-feature vs. research-artifact framing is explored
At least 5 of the 8 standard discovery items above are explored

At Closing (after Phase 2 RED-TEAM exits), a discovery artifact is written to docs/socrates/discover/<slug>.md following the template (outcomes, parked shapes, open axes, externally-sourced constraints with source annotations, discovery log)

evals.expected_output: Full discovery and red-team run even though the problem is small. Discovery artifact records outcomes, parked shapes, and any externally-sourced constraints. Chunk decomposition does not happen in /discover (chunking moved to /solution post-PR 2). Build-vs-buy evaluation (surfacing Todoist / Linear / Apple Reminders / plain text file etc.) lives in /solution's RESEARCH phase, not /discover.
expectations: Phase 1 (DISCOVER) executes with at least one Socratic clarifying question
The skill does NOT propose chunk decomposition during /discover (chunking is /solution's responsibility post-PR 2)
Phase 2 (RED-TEAM) executes and announces the mode shift to red-team explicitly
Phase 2 produces at least one finding of any severity (CRITICAL, DISCUSS, or MINOR)

At Closing (after Phase 2 RED-TEAM exits), a discovery artifact is written with outcomes, parked shapes, and any externally-sourced constraints — NOT with chunk sections (chunks are written by /solution)
If the operator named a specific technology (e.g., 'React', 'Postgres') without citing an external source, the technology is parked as a shape, not recorded as a constraint

evals.prompt: We need a platform with auth, billing, a marketplace, and analytics
evals.expected_output: The /discover skill explores outcomes for each subsystem and parks named technologies as shapes unless externally sourced. Chunk decomposition with dependencies does not happen in /discover — that work moves to /solution's Phase 1 CHUNK, which consumes /discover's outcomes + parked shapes. Build-vs-buy evaluation (Auth0/Clerk for auth, Stripe for billing, etc.) lives in /solution's Phase 3 RESEARCH.
expectations: Phase 1 (DISCOVER) executes with at least one Socratic clarifying question per subsystem (auth, billing, marketplace, analytics)
The skill does NOT propose chunk decomposition during /discover — chunking moved to /solution's Phase 1 CHUNK post-PR 2
Outcome questions are surfaced for each subsystem (e.g., 'who is authenticating?', 'what currencies / regions for billing?', 'who lists on the marketplace?', 'what analytics dimensions matter?')
Named technologies that surface (e.g., 'OAuth', 'Stripe', 'Snowflake') trigger Tech-D's verifiability rule — the skill asks for the external source and parks them as shapes if none is cited
At Closing (after Phase 2 RED-TEAM exits), a discovery artifact is written with outcomes per subsystem, parked shapes (including any named technologies), and any externally-sourced constraints
The artifact's parked shapes section preserves the named technologies as input for /solution's Phase 0 SHAPE-DISCOVER and Phase 3 RESEARCH to evaluate

evals.prompt: Build a REST API using Express with Postgres and deploy to AWS ECS
evals.expected_output: The skill treats this as an over-specified prompt. Tech-D's verifiability rule fires on every named specific (REST, Express, Postgres, AWS ECS), asking for the external source — and each specific is parked as a shape unless the operator cites one of the five external categories. Tech-B fires with alternative framings across the full complexity spectrum including a credible no-build frame. Build-vs-buy evaluation of managed/serverless alternatives (Supabase, Render, Railway, Fly.io, Neon) happens later in /solution's Phase 3 RESEARCH, not /discover.
expectations: Phase 1 (DISCOVER) executes with at least one Socratic clarifying question
Tech-D's verifiability rule fires on 'Express' — the skill asks for the external source (regulator, contract, deployed system, prior empirical result, or factual measurement)
Tech-D's verifiability rule fires on 'Postgres' — the skill asks for the external source
Tech-D's verifiability rule fires on 'AWS ECS' — the skill asks for the external source
At least 2 of the 3 named specifics (Express, Postgres, AWS ECS) trigger Tech-D's verifiability rule
When the operator cannot cite an external source for a specific, the skill parks it under '## Parked shapes' with an outcome-question — it does NOT lock it in as a constraint
Tech-B (alternative framings) fires at least once during Phase 1
Tech-B presents exactly 4 framings: a complex frame, a middle-build frame, a low-build frame, and a no-build frame
Tech-B's option 4 (no-build frame) is a credible no-code path the operator could actually take — not just 'a smaller version of frame 1'

At Closing (after Phase 2 RED-TEAM exits), a discovery artifact is written whose '## Parked shapes' section contains the named specifics (Express, Postgres, AWS ECS) with outcome-questions, ready for /solution's Phase 0 SHAPE-DISCOVER to resolve

evals.prompt: [/solution smoke test] Given a synthetic discovery.md with outcomes 'sub-100ms p95 latency' and 'support 10k concurrent users' and parked shapes '[Redis caching layer]' and '[Postgres read replicas]' — run /solution's SHAPE-DISCOVER phase.
evals.expected_output: /solution's SHAPE-DISCOVER phase reads the discovery artifact, walks the parked-shapes list, and runs Tech-D's preference-path classification on each (constraint / candidate / default-to-test). Tech-B fires 1-2x with alternative shape framings across the complexity spectrum. The eventual solution artifact's Discovery → Solution mapping table contains a row for every outcome, and the Parked shapes resolution table contains a row for every parked shape.
expectations: Phase 0 (SHAPE-DISCOVER) executes — the skill reads the discovery artifact and the parked-shapes ledger before doing anything else

For each parked shape ('Redis caching layer', 'Postgres read replicas'), Tech-D's classification fires: constraint (lock in with external-source citation) / candidate (test against alternatives) / default-to-test (run Tech-B's no-build framing)
Tech-B fires at least once during SHAPE-DISCOVER with 4 shape framings spanning the complexity spectrum (Complex / Middle / Low / No-build, where No-build = adopt existing tool)

The solution artifact's '## Discovery → Solution mapping' table contains a row for the 'sub-100ms p95 latency' outcome mapped to ≥1 chunk whose problem statement qualitatively names the outcome (per G5)

The solution artifact's '## Discovery → Solution mapping' table contains a row for the 'support 10k concurrent users' outcome mapped to ≥1 chunk whose problem statement qualitatively names the outcome (per G5)

The solution artifact's '## Parked shapes resolution' table contains a row for 'Redis caching layer' with a Resolution value of exactly one of `Resolved` / `Dropped` / `Carried forward as open shape` (per G6 and the solution-artifact template)

The solution artifact's '## Parked shapes resolution' table contains a row for 'Postgres read replicas' with a Resolution value of exactly one of `Resolved` / `Dropped` / `Carried forward as open shape` (per G6 and the solution-artifact template)
If any parked shape is marked 'Dropped', the row gives a specific reason (not 'TBD', not 'not needed')
